title: Build a Thriving Community Garden from a Vacant Lot
projectPace: Moderate - steady hands-on work with breaks
description: Help turn a neglected corner lot into a flourishing food garden that feeds 40+ neighborhood families every season.
organizerBackstory: This lot has sat vacant for nine years, collecting waste and dividing the block. Together we'll build 12 raised beds, a pollinator border, and a tool-share shed — converting blight into a source of fresh produce and belonging for a neighborhood that lives in a food desert.
impactQuoteText: I hadn't grown anything since I left my mother's farm 40 years ago. Now my grandchildren pull carrots from a bed I helped build. This garden gave the whole block its mornings back.
duties: Building raised timber garden beds for community planting
Gardening and planting over 20 organic saplings & greens
Spreading nutrient-rich soil and organic wood mulch layers
Collaborating in volunteer teams to complete layout structures
requirements: 16+ (under 18 needs guardian consent)
English or Spanish spoken on-site
Able to lift ~20 lbs and work standing

socialProof.communityText: Amara O. and 15 others from your community have signed up.

coordinators.name: Amara Okafor
coordinators.bio: Master gardener, 8 years building urban food systems across the East Bay.

title: Mentoring Students
projectPace: Moderate - steady pace with group breaks
description: Spend a few hours mentoring students and helping them build confidence through reading.
organizerBackstory: In our district, early reading proficiency has declined by 20% over the last three years. Local schools lack the resources to provide individual attention, leaving struggling students behind. We launched this literacy program at Pratham MP Education Group to pair eager young learners with friendly mentors, reading confidence.
impactQuoteText: My son was terrified of reading in front of his class. After just three weeks with his mentor, he started reading bedtime stories to his little sister. The difference is night and day.
duties: Read chapters aloud alongside elementary school students to build flow
Help learners sound out difficult words and define unfamiliar terms
Encourage active listening and self-correction during reading sessions
Provide friendly, encouraging feedback to boost student confidence
requirements: Patient and encouraging attitude towards young learners
Strong reading skills in local language
Background check clearance (completed online)

socialProof.communityText: anita, sunita, rahul, vivek and 5 others from your community

coordinators.name: Sofia Ramirez
coordinators.bio: Your day-of point of contact for questions, breaks, and accommodations.

backedTrusted.verifiedStatus: Pratham MP Education Group · verified organizer

title: Sort and Pack Medical Supplies
projectPace: Moderate - steady pace with group breaks
description: Help sort and package essential medicines and primary diagnostic kits for rural clinics.
organizerBackstory: Rural clinics in the outlying sectors of Gwalior serve thousands of families daily but struggle with stock management and sorting supplies. Expired diagnostics and unorganized medical supplies slow down critical healthcare delivery. We sort and bundle these resources so frontline doctors have immediate access to what they need.
impactQuoteText: Having sorted packages means our triage nurse doesn't waste hours looking for basic diagnostics. It saves lives when speed is everything.
impactQuoteAuthor: Dr. Ananya Joshi, rural health director
duties: Sort incoming medical supplies by product category and expiration date
Verify packaging integrity of primary diagnostic kits and bandages
Pack sorted items into regional clinic distribution bins securely
Log packed box counts on the volunteer inventory sheet before dispatch
requirements: Detail-oriented and follows strict safety protocols
Able to stand and sort items at a sorting table
Sanitize hands and wear protective gloves provided on-site

socialProof.communityText: vikram and 0 others from your community

coordinators.name: Sofia Ramirez
coordinators.bio: Your day-of point of contact for questions, breaks, and accommodations.

title: Evening Shelter Food Distribution
projectPace: Moderate - steady pace with group breaks
description: Help distribute warm dinner packets and verify overnight counts at community transit shelters.
organizerBackstory: During cold winter nights and severe weather, hundreds of transient laborers sleep at transit shelters without warm meals. Hunger compounds the physical toll of living on the streets. Our evening shelter run delivers freshly prepared, hot meals directly to these transit points, ensuring no neighbor goes to sleep hungry.
impactQuoteText: A warm meal at the end of a long, exhausting day is a blessing. It shows us that Gwalior still cares about its workers.
duties: Assemble hot food packets into thermal delivery containers
Distribute meal boxes to transient laborers at designated transit shelters
Verify and log nightly count of served meals at each stop
Collect and recycle empty food packet packaging on-site
requirements: Empathetic, respectful, and friendly demeanor
Comfortable working outdoors in the evening
Must coordinate with the transport team for logistics

socialProof.communityText: rohan, priya, sanjay, sneha and 0 others from your community

coordinators.name: Sofia Ramirez
coordinators.bio: Your day-of point of contact for questions, breaks, and accommodations.

backedTrusted.verifiedStatus: Robin Hood Army Gwalior · verified organizer

title: Miyawaki Plantation drive
projectPace: Moderate - steady pace with group breaks
description: Help dig, soil-prep, and plant saplings in the urban Miyawaki forest zone to combat summer temperatures.
organizerBackstory: Urban concrete surfaces absorb heat, raising summer temperatures to extreme levels. Deforestation has stripped away natural canopies. We are planting native Miyawaki forests inside vacant public blocks to restore biodiversity, lower temperatures by up to 2°C, and bring clean air back to our families.
impactQuoteText: We planted over 100 saplings in our neighborhood block. Now, birds have returned, and the area feels cooler and livelier already.
impactQuoteAuthor: Sanjay V., local resident & tree warden
duties: Dig planting holes of appropriate depth for native saplings
Mix organic manure and soil compost into tree beds
Plant native tree starts and secure them with wooden supports
Water the newly planted zone and spread protective mulch layers
requirements: Able to perform physical outdoor work (shoveling, planting)
Wear sturdy closed-toe shoes and clothes that can get dirty
Bring your own water bottle to stay hydrated

socialProof.communityText: Be the first from your community to sign up

coordinators.name: Sofia Ramirez
coordinators.bio: Your day-of point of contact for questions, breaks, and accommodations.

title: Review Crop Hailstorm Relief Requests
projectPace: Self-paced - complete tasks at your convenience
description: Review field reports and remote photos of hailstorm damage to approve seed subsidy vouchers for farmers.
organizerBackstory: Severe hailstorms frequently destroy crops in Gwalior's rural belt, leaving small farmers with massive losses and zero seed funds for the next season. Relief seed voucher approval is often delayed by slow paperwork verification. By reviewing field damage reports online, we speed up the distribution of relief seed vouchers.
impactQuoteText: Getting my seed relief approved in days instead of months meant I didn't miss the monsoon sowing season. It saved my farm.
duties: Access the field damage photo logs via the disaster management portal
Cross-reference damage claims with uploaded remote survey coordinates
Verify relief seed eligibility based on the hailstorm severity criteria
Approve or escalate claim requests to the district relief officer
requirements: Requires a computer with a stable internet connection
Attend a 30-minute online training session before starting
High attention to detail to prevent fraudulent claims

socialProof.communityText: anita, sneha and 0 others from your community

coordinators.name: Sofia Ramirez
coordinators.bio: Your day-of point of contact for questions, breaks, and accommodations.

backedTrusted.verifiedStatus: MP Disaster Management Authority · verified organizer

title: Digitize Textbooks for Visually Impaired
projectPace: Self-paced - complete tasks at your convenience
description: Read and verify audio transcriptions of secondary school textbooks for visually impaired college students.
organizerBackstory: Over 40% of visually impaired students drop out of secondary education due to a lack of accessible textbooks. Traditional audio recording is slow and expensive. We digitize text and verify audio transcriptions to provide high-quality, searchable screen-reader audio files, giving every student equal learning opportunities.
impactQuoteText: Having screen-reader access to my biology textbooks allowed me to prepare for my board exams independently. I passed with honors!
duties: Listen to automated audio text segments via the transcription portal
Compare the spoken audio with the printed textbook page script
Edit spelling, pronunciation, and flow errors in the transcription log
Submit verified audio files to the student accessible library
requirements: Requires a computer with a stable internet connection and headphones
Excellent listening and spelling skills
Commitment of at least 3 hours per week

socialProof.communityText: rahul, priya, mohit, neha, sanjay and 0 others from your community

coordinators.name: Sofia Ramirez
coordinators.bio: Your day-of point of contact for questions, breaks, and accommodations.

backedTrusted.verifiedStatus: NAB India Digitization Unit · verified organizer

title: Vaccination Campaign Social Media Design
projectPace: Self-paced - complete tasks at your convenience
description: Draft graphics and local language posters for stray dog anti-rabies vaccination drives.
organizerBackstory: Stray dog rabies outbreaks pose a serious health risk to both street animals and residents in Gwalior. Fear leads to cruelty towards stray dogs. We run vaccination and reflection collar drives to protect street animals, and create graphics and local posters to raise awareness and encourage compassion.
impactQuoteText: Reflection collars and vaccine awareness drives have significantly reduced accidents and street conflicts in our sector.
impactQuoteAuthor: Priya K., community stray welfare group lead
duties: Design high-contrast posters explaining local vaccination schedules
Draft engaging social media graphics promoting animal welfare
Translate safety instructions into simple Hindi flyer layouts
Share draft graphics with the animal welfare site team for approval
requirements: Experience with Canva or basic design tools
Empathetic stance on stray animal welfare
Access to design software (remote volunteering)

socialProof.communityText: Be the first from your community to sign up

coordinators.name: Amara Okafor
coordinators.bio: Master gardener, 8 years building urban food systems across the East Bay.

title: Verify Clean Water Tap Locations
projectPace: Moderate - steady pace with group breaks
description: Verify water dispenser pin coordinates on the map for summer drinking points.
organizerBackstory: Our community water points are critical for providing safe, clean drinking water to thousands of Gwalior residents during hot summer days. Incorrect GPS pin coordinates lead to logistics delays and broken dispensers. By cross-referencing and mapping these locations, you make clean water accessible to all.
impactQuoteText: Verifying the dispenser locations ensures that when a tap breaks, our repair crew gets there within an hour. It keeps clean water flowing.
duties: Access the local mapping console via the volunteer dashboard
Cross-reference community submissions of summer drinking taps
Verify the geographical pins match public street locations
Approve mapped water points for listing in the mobile locator
requirements: Able to stand and walk during the shift

socialProof.communityText: anita, sunita, rahul and 0 others from your community
